title: Neomammillaria wrightii
intro: Neomammillaria wrightii, also known as Wright's pincushion cactus, is a species of cactus native to Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Neomammillaria wrightii include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Neomammillaria wrightii is classified within the family Cactaceae. This classification places it among other cacti plants. The genus Neomammillaria is now considered synonymous with Mammillaria, so Neomammillaria wrightii is also classified as Mammillaria wrightii. At the species level, it is recognized as a distinct species within the Mammillaria genus. The classification of Neomammillaria wrightii is subject to ongoing research and updates in botanical taxonomy.
uses: Neomammillaria wrightii is a species of cactus that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Traditionally, the plant's extracts have been used to treat a range of ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal uses and to confirm its safety for human consumption. Further studies are required to determine the efficacy and optimal dosage of Neomammillaria wrightii for medicinal purposes.

The stems are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and inflammation.
